calendar.matters:
Motion For Reconsideration And Revocation Of The Order Granting Elizabeth R. Papera'S Petition To Enforce Support Judgment Filed 9/09/09 And Amended Order Filed 9/11/09
Off Calendar Nov-04-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Susan Kaye for Wells Fargo Bank. Niki Okcu for and with Elizabeth Papera.

Ms. Kaye began by requesting a statement of decision as set forth on the record. The Court stated that it was of the opinion to grant the motion. Ms. Okcu presented argument to support her position stating that the notice issue was laid out in their papers, and that they did their best to give notice. Ms. Kaye presented argument to support her position. Ms. Okcu stated that there is an order in place, that should be enforced, and that this isn't the proper venue. The Court stated that it was inclined to set aside this Court's order based upon lack of notice/improper notice. Ms. Okcu presented additional argument. Ms. Kaye stated that if the orders are being revoked then she is requesting that their motion be granted, that Ms. Okcu can refile/renotice, and that notice should include notice to the contingency beneficiaries. The Court noted that it would not grant the request to modify the method of notice but it would grant everything else. The Court granted the petition, (signing the order for the same in open court). Ms. Kaye withdrew her request for a Statment of Decision.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli
